title: VIT Config
description: Configuration for VIT (Version It!) — version and release manager.

semantic.description: If true, the changelog is fully regenerated from git tags (semantic mode). If false, it is managed manually.

git.description: Git configuration for commits, releases and branches.

defaultCommitMessage.description: Default commit message for regular commit operations.

releaseCommitMessage.description: Default commit message when performing a release.

changelogCommitMessage.description: Default commit message when updating the changelog.

strict.description: If true, releases are blocked from branches not listed in 'releaseBranches'. If false, only a warning is shown.

releaseBranches.description: List of branches from which releases are allowed.

rollbackStrategy.description: Rollback strategy. 'revert' creates a new commit that undoes changes (history preserved). 'reset' rewrites history (requires force push).

envFile.description: Path to the environment variables file to be loaded during operations.

projects.description: List of projects managed by VIT. Each project has its own package.json and can be versioned independently.

id.description: Unique project identifier. Used in CLI with --projects.

label.description: Human-readable project name shown in the interface.

path.description: Relative path to the project directory (where its package.json is located).

tagPrefix.description: Prefix used for git tags (e.g. 'v' generates tags like 'v1.2.3').

preActions.description: Actions executed BEFORE the main operation (commit or release).

postActions.description: Actions executed AFTER the main operation (commit or release).

onEvents.description: Events on which this action is triggered.

items.description: 'commit' = triggered on regular commits. 'release' = triggered on releases.

label.description: Human-readable action name shown in the interface.

continueOnError.description: If true, VIT continues even if this action fails. If false, the operation is aborted.

showOutput.description: If true, the command output is printed to the terminal.

pipelineAction.description: Post-operation action. Can include a pipeline of steps that capture environment variables.

continueOnError.description: If true, VIT continues even if this action fails.

showOutput.description: If true, the command output is printed to the terminal.

command.description: Shell command to execute (can use variables captured from the pipeline: ${VAR_NAME}).

pipeline.description: List of preceding steps that capture values into environment variables, available in 'command' as ${VAR_NAME}.

items.description: Pipeline step that runs a command and captures its output.

command.description: Shell command whose standard output (stdout) is captured.

captureAs.description: Name of the environment variable where the command output is stored (e.g. 'NEW_VERSION' → available as ${NEW_VERSION}).

continueOnError.description: If true, the pipeline continues even if this step fails.
